phase1: Map all routes: method + path + handler — identify missing input validation or auth guards
Trace middleware stack: order matters (auth before business logic, error handler last)
Document error handling: are all async route handlers wrapped or using express-async-errors?
Identify database access patterns: ORM, raw queries, connection pooling setup
Check for secrets/env vars: hardcoded values, missing validation of required env at startup
Assess route file size — large files often indicate missing controller/service layering
phase3: Validate all input at the boundary with zod, joi, or Fastify's built-in schema validation
Separate routes, controllers, services, and repositories — keep handler functions under 20 lines
Use a centralized error handler middleware; never send stack traces to clients in production
Wrap async route handlers consistently (express-async-errors or try/catch + next(err))
Return consistent JSON shapes: { data, error, meta } — document with OpenAPI/JSDoc
Use dependency injection or factory functions for services to enable unit testing without I/O
phase4: Unit-test service layer functions with mocked repository/DB dependencies
Integration-test routes with supertest — assert status codes, response shapes, and error responses
Test middleware in isolation by calling them directly with mock req/res/next objects
Test error handling paths: invalid input, missing auth, DB failure simulation
Use a test database or in-memory SQLite for integration tests; never hit production DB
